channel.title: iLearn
channel.description: A personal learning podcast. Two hosts go deep on business, real estate, AI, philosophy, and the ideas worth understanding: built to survive your follow-up questions, with a little wisdom on the way out.
channel.itunes:author: iLearn
channel.itunes:summary: Two hosts go deep on business, real estate, AI, philosophy, and the ideas worth understanding.

item.title: 04 · Visual Edition: The OKC Flipper Plan ($200k, Full Time, 14 Possible)
item.description: A listener letter from Mark in Edmond: $200k, full-time, his own trade skills, and a goal of 5-10 flips in 5 years in Oklahoma City. We prove the goal is too humble: the deal math ($120k buy, $30k rehab, ~$41k kept per flip), the cash edge, the Oklahoma licensing law that takes two of his five skills off the table, a month-by-month first flip, the three-phase plan to 14 flips and ~$457k, and every pitfall including the one about insuring the machine: him. Recap included. Closes with Proverbs 14:23. Follow-along slide deck (auto-syncs to the audio): https://dumbsaucey.github.io/ilearn-podcast/decks/04-okc-flipper-plan-deck.html

item.title: Special Edition: $200k, Five Years, Ten Houses: The Edmond, Oklahoma Plan
item.description: A full deep dive: can $200,000 cash become 5-10 properties in Edmond, OK in five years? Real 2026 market data, the complete deal math (ARV, the 70% rule, hard money, BRRRR, DSCR), a step-by-step playbook, the team and relationships to build, fundraising channels, the 5-year financial model, Oklahoma-specific traps (hail, clay soil, abstracts), and every shortfall: including the one nobody talks about, negative cash flow per door. Closes with Proverbs 21:5. Follow-along slide deck (auto-syncs to the audio): https://dumbsaucey.github.io/ilearn-podcast/decks/2026-06-12-edmond-special-deck.html

item.title: Reading the Housing Market: When to Buy, When to Sell
item.description: The three-number scoreboard that tells you whether any zip code favors buyers or sellers: months of supply, days on market, and sale-to-list with price cuts. Why each works, where to find it, the traps inside it, and a flip example where ignoring the numbers costs twenty five thousand dollars. Closes with Proverbs 24:27.

item.title: How to Value a Small Business (ETA Deep-Dive)
item.description: Entrepreneurship through acquisition, from the ground floor to the bank's underwriting desk: SDE, add-backs, why the multiple is a risk score, DSCR, and a worked example buying a plumbing company with current SBA rules. Closes with Luke 14:28 on counting the cost.
